title: Graduate
description:
Overview

WELCOME TO SITA

We're the team that keeps airports moving, airlines flying smoothly, and borders open. Our tech and communication breakthroughs are the foundation of the global air travel industry.

You'll find us at 95% of international hubs. We partner closely with over 2,500 transportation and government clients, each with their own unique needs and challenges. Our goal is to find fresh solutions and cutting-edge tech to make their operations run like clockwork. Want to be a part of something big?

Are you ready to love your job? The adventure begins right here, with you, at SITA.

About The Role & Team

SITA India is on the lookout for recent graduates to join our SkyStar Program within the Procurement function, initially for a 12-month period, with the potential for a permanent role.

Role

Your main tasks will involve supporting the manufacturing procurement and the sourcing specialists and provide supporting data and MI to create more informed procurements as well as play a hands-on role in direct sourcing activity, obtaining quotes, supporting RFx`s and E Sourcing initiatives, providing assistance under supervision of the Manufacturing Procurement lead, and ensuring the smooth operation of procurement. This entry-level opportunity offers a chance to dive into various activities within our fast-paced global community:


Connecting with Diverse Individuals: Collaborate locally and globally.
Building Skills for Your Career: Develop essential skills tailored to your job stream.
Joining Corporate Projects: Contribute to projects shaping the future of our industry.


Early career talent is our future, and we're thrilled to have you join impactful missions alongside our experts, gaining valuable experiences. We can't wait to witness the incredible things you'll accomplish with us!

Empowered People, Reinventing Travel

What You’ll Do


Support Operative Project Procurement by assisting in day‑to‑day purchasing activities for ongoing projects, including data preparation, order follow‑up, supplier coordination, and documentation management
Collaborate with project managers and engineering teams to ensure materials and services are procured in line with project timelines, specifications, and quality requirements.
Follow up with suppliers on project‑critical deliveries, order confirmations, documentation requests, and issue resolution to support smooth project execution.
Support procurement transformation initiatives, contributing to process improvements, digital tools adoption, standardization, and increased operational efficiency within the Procurement team.
Ensure compliance with internal procurement policies, documentation standards, and approval workflows while maintaining accurate records.


Qualifications

KNOWLEDGE & SKILLS


Strong analytical skills with attention to detail
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word)
Ability to learn quickly and work in a dynamic team environment
High level of motivation, curiosity, and willingness to take initiative
Basic understanding of procurement, supply chain, or manufacturing processes
Knowledge of e‑sourcing tools or ERP platforms


Education & Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in supply chain management, Business Administration, Engineering, Commerce, or a related field.


What We Offer

We're all about diversity. We operate in 200 countries and speak 60 different languages and cultures. We're really proud of our inclusive environment. Our offices are comfortable and fun places to work, and we make sure you get to work from home too. Find out what it's like to join our team and take a step closer to your best life ever.

🏡 Flex Week: Work from home up to 2 days/week (depending on your team's needs)

⏰ Flex Day: Make your workday suit your life and plans.

🌎 Flex-Location: Take up to 30 days a year to work from any location in the world.

🌿 Employee Wellbeing: We have got you covered with our Employee Assistance Program (EAP), for you and your dependents 24/7, 365 days/year. We also offer Champion Health - a personalized platform that supports a range of wellbeing needs.

🚀 Professional Development: At SITA, we believe growth fuels innovation. Our learning ecosystem offers access to world-class platforms and programs designed to help you thrive. From LinkedIn Learning, Microsoft's Enterprise Skills Initiative, and Airport Council International -available to all employees-to specialized solutions like Pluralsight for technology upskilling, Harvard Business Publishing for people leadership, Stanford for strategic development and many others, we align learning opportunities with your Development Plan and our business priorities. Your development journey is supported every step of the way.

🙌 Competitive Benefits: Competitive benefits that make sense with both your local market and employment status.

SITA is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We value a diverse workforce. In support of our Employment Equity Program, we encourage women, aboriginal people, members of visible minorities, and/or persons with disabilities to apply and self-identify in the application process.

title: Area Manager
description:
Description

Amazon is looking for bright leaders to lead large non-exempt teams across our fulfillment network! This is not a corporate, office-based position. This is a full-time position located within one of our Amazon fulfillment centers, sort centers, delivery stations or other operations buildings.

Amazon provides extensive training and development for entry level managers to become exceptional people leaders. Being an Area Manager means being on the front lines of our customer promise, ensuring your team delivers customers’ orders on time, by leading a large team of Amazon Associates in one of our fulfillment locations. You will have an opportunity to invest in others and develop a collaborative leadership style, while utilizing the tools, processes, and operations that have helped create the most customer-centric company on Earth.

What is fulfillment you ask? Fulfillment is how we refer to completing or fulfilling a customer’s Amazon.com order and the acts of picking, packing, shipping and delivering their order to meet or exceed their expectations! Our Operation’s workflow can be broken to three major lanes: first mile - where the product is housed and ready for your order; second mile - where your order is hauled to your area; and last mile - when the product is delivered to your door.

Please note that all lanes have slight building variations, but one thing is constant, our vision and dedication to the customer. As a reminder, our fulfillment centers are fast paced environments where standing, walking, and having on-the-production floor conversations are common as a visible supply chain leader!

As an Area Manager, you will have the opportunity to lead and develop your own team of Amazon Associates in a distribution warehouse environment. Your main focus will be motivating, mentoring, and managing your team to meet targeted goals. As a people manager, you will ensure that the team of Amazon associates has all the tools needed to succeed and maintain the highest levels of safety, quality, and attendance performance at work.

Key job responsibilities


Communicate policies and act as the primary information source for your team- maintaining compliance, consistency, and taking corrective action when needed
Create, manage and support recognition programs
Support safety programs and OSHA compliance to ensure a safe work environment
Ensure procedures are followed for building security and product loss prevention
Partner with the management team to establish and maintain quality control standards
Develop performance goals and targets to achieve customer demand and ensure accuracy and quality


Must be able to perform the following tasks, with or without reasonable accommodation:


Stand/walk for up to 10-12 hours
Access all areas of building including ladders, stairs, gangways and shipping docks safely
Walk in/and around all areas of the warehouse with great frequency
Willing and able to work shift patterns that may include nights, weekends, and/or holidays based on business needs, regularly . Note that there is a high chance that your shift may overlap into part of the weekend or be overnight.
Regularly bend, lift, stretch and reach both below the waist and above the head
Lift and move items up to 49 pounds


A day in the life

As an Operations Area Manager I, you lead teams by driving work from preparation through execution. Each shift, you set clear priorities, motivate associates and actively support operations while conducting safety observations and maintaining engagement. You balance operational demands with creating a safe, energized and inclusive environment. You manage daily task allocation, solve defined problems and communicate decisions to senior leaders. You deliver customer results by managing resources, auditing workflows and ensuring SLA adherence. Through coaching, 1:1s and performance evaluations, you develop talent, support career growth and build a cohesive, high-performing team.

About The Team

The Operations Team is dedicated to providing exceptional service to our customer, Amazon business units. As leaders, we pride ourselves on creating a team environment that lifts all members up. Our philosophy is simple: We all rise together. We combine diverse skills, experiences, and perspectives to deliver innovative solutions and continuously improve our service. Our strength lies in our unity, mutual support, and shared commitment to success. Together, we're building a culture of excellence that benefits our customers, our team, and the entire organization. In the Operations Team, every member plays a crucial role in our collective journey towards greatness.

Basic Qualifications


4+ years of professional or military experience
Bachelor's degree or equivalent, or experience in professional or military settings
Experience in project management
1+ years of data analysis in the facilities maintenance program, specifically in asset management and spare part optimization experience
Manage end-to-end IT asset lifecycle including procurement, tagging, deployment, tracking, maintenance, and disposal in compliance with company policies.
Coordinate device deployment and refresh activities, ensuring timely delivery, configuration, and handover to end users.
Oversee warehouse operations including receipt, storage, dispatch, and reconciliation of IT hardware and peripherals.
Ensure proper handling, storage, and security of IT assets to minimize loss, damage, or non-compliance.
Collaborate with IT, procurement, and vendors to support operational efficiency and business continuity.


Preferred Qualifications


1+ years of performance metrics, process improvement or lean techniques experience
1+ years of employee and performance management experience
2+ years of military experience


Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

title: BSA - Assistant Manager - Hyderabad
description:
Summary

Position Summary

Senior Business Systems Analyst (BSA) Location: Hyderabad, India Are you passionate about technology and interested in joining a community of collaborative colleagues who respectfully and courageously seek to challenge the status quo? If so, read on to learn more about an exciting opportunity with Deloitte’s Technology (DT). We are curious and life-long learners focused on technology and innovation. Work you’ll do


Responsible for leading conversations with stakeholders and translating technical discussions and directions into functional business requirements through detailed acceptance criterias.
Responsible for implementing organizational delivery standards, practitioner training, work scope management and the continuous enhancement of BA practitioner delivery capabilities.
Responsible for ensuring all business analysis and quality assurance work products adhere to the practices and standards defined by Community of Practice and project leaders.
Responsible for active participation in appropriate professional Community of Practice forums.
Responsible for determining level of effort estimations, perform task execution and meeting established project milestones.
Responsible for the timeliness and accuracy of necessary escalations, producing and maintaining applicable project metrics and status reporting within a project team.
Collaborates with technical delivery team members to maximize and leverage the understanding of business solution functional requirements and non-functional requirements.
Responsible for the documentation of formal requirements, use cases, user stories, test plans, test cases, test scripts and test suites which ensure that the software solutions developed and tested are fit for purpose, meet business needs and are operationally sustainable.
Responsible for complying with time reporting and other mandated organizational and corporate policies. Duties & Responsibilities:
Understands business strategy and objectives by studying the business function, gathering information, and evaluating output requirements and formats. Elicits and documents functional requirements and test strategies for technical solutions to meet business needs.
Possesses advanced level understanding of BA, QA/QC professional standards, business processes, workflows, methodologies and leading practices.
Ensure for traceability and optimum coverage of business requirements.
Collaborates with application developers, designers, and architects to ensure a shared understanding of the business model and the detailed function requirements.
Establishes the estimation of requirements documentation and testing efforts for various projects and initiatives.
Provides senior management and project management with regular updates on deliverable timeline and project status.
Responsible for conducting Design Thinking sessions to gain insights into end user and customer needs. Manages the Product Backlog for a product, working with the Technical Delivery Manager to prioritize requirements, define and manage scope and sprints for a project
Works with Technical Delivery Manager and end users to plan and execute User Acceptance Testing.
Participates in reviews of plans associated with project execution.
Responds proactively to both business and project issues and escalates appropriately.
Ensures communication of Change Management activities and confirms solution and schedule adaptability
Performs other job-related duties as assigned. Qualifications:
Minimum 7-8 years of work experience as a Business Analyst.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Business Administration or equivalent education. Required Technical Skills:
Strong relationship building and consulting skills. Fosters a climate conducive to establishing positive working relationships with clients and cross functional team members
Strong communication, technical and remote collaboration skills are a must
Advanced knowledge of Agile methodology
Excellent organization, analytical and planning skills
Experience in dealing with multiple projects and cross-functional teams and ability to coordinate across teams in a large matrix organization environment
Familiarity with SharePoint and automated QA tools is a plus
Mobile application design and testing experience is a plus
Understanding Java, HTML, Microsoft Office and test automation scripting methodologies is a plus
Knowledge of designing wireframe tools like Figma is a plus. Preferred:
Experience working with Agile/SAFe development processes
Experience in products like Azure Devops/ServiceNow etc.
ISTQB, CBAP or CCBA certifications are a plus
Scrum master certification is a plus. How you’ll grow At Deloitte, our professional development plan focuses on helping people at every level of their career to help them to identify and use their strengths to do their best work every day. title: GTM Associate
description:
An accelerated Leadership Track for fresh graduates and recent graduates (0-2 years) who want to build, learn, and lead early: compressing ~5 years of career growth into ~2.




The Opportunity

Designed for an extremely limited set of exceptional fresh graduates stepping into their early full-time role, this position offers a non-linear, accelerated path to leadership. The Founder’s Office – GTM Associate is a high-intensity track built around real ownership, steep learning curves, and close collaboration with founders and senior leaders — compressing ~5 years of career growth into ~2.

You’ll work on high-impact initiatives across growth, strategy, customer engagement, and market expansion. Top performers quickly take on ownership, lead teams, and drive initiatives across geographies. This is not a conventional entry-level job. This is a launchpad for future business leaders.




What you'll do

Work directly with founders and senior leaders on GTM strategy, execution, and experimentation
Drive market expansion and international go-to-market initiatives
Own cross-functional projects across sales, marketing, customer success, partnerships, and product
Lead revenue growth and funnel optimization initiatives
Analyze markets, customers, and performance data to identify growth opportunities
Engage with global customers to uncover pain points and build customer-first solutions
Support and lead new launches, pilots, and strategic projects
Collaborate across geographies and functions to deliver outcomes
Take end-to-end ownership of initiatives and be accountable for results
Over time, you will move from: Execution → Ownership → Market/Function Leadership → Team Leadership / P&L Responsibility
Our expectation is clear: 




Within ~2 years, high performers will be operating at a team-lead level. This program is built for people who want accelerated responsibility, not gradual hand-holding.




Who we're looking for

We seek outliers and you might be a fit if you have:

Undergraduate degree in any discipline from a Tier 1 institution, completed by June 2026 (or final examinations completed by then). Open to recent graduates with 0–2 years of experience
Willingness to commit to a minimum of two years with the organization
Strong spoken and written English communication
High learning agility, curiosity, and initiative-taking ability
Entrepreneurial, ownership-driven mindset—you see problems as opportunities
Strong business aptitude, logical reasoning, and comfort with numbers and data
Structured, strategic thinking with exceptional attention to detail
Proven problem-solving skills and customer empathy
Passionate hustler mentality with the drive to execute, not just plan
Excited by ambiguity, high expectations, and real responsibility
Aspiration to become a future business leader, not just a functional specialist
Hunger to work on cutting-edge, industry-leading problems
Bonus: Proficiency in a foreign language




About DeltaX

Founded in 2012, DeltaX is an AI & Automation company driving transformation primarily in the area of Marketing & Advertising. Our flagship product is a data-driven digital advertising platform designed for agencies and advertisers to seamlessly plan, create, buy, track, attribute, and report campaigns across search, social, display RTB, mobile, video, and other digital channels. Its cutting-edge technology empowers marketing teams worldwide with deep insights, automated ROI-based spend optimisation, and the ability to execute award-winning, technology-driven campaigns. The scope of our offering has compounded in width and depth with AI and automation being at the forefront of it. 




Evaluation Process

Getting to Know You + Assignment Submission via Google Form
Interviews
Offer Roll-out

title: Associate Data Scientist (BI - Reporting)
description:
Level: Associate (L3)

Role: BI Reporting – MSO Ops

Mio Overview

T The Marketing Science Operations (MSO) vertical at Amgen is the operational backbone that ensures accurate, timely, governed, and analytics ready marketing data across all digital channels. MSO enables the MIO (Marketing Investment & Optimization), and broader Decision Sciences teams to run self-service analytics, generate insights, and execute campaigns reliably.

MSO focuses on data readiness, governance, pipeline operations, vendor accountability, and automation with the goal of delivering clean, standardized, and insight-ready datasets.

Role Summary

The Associate – MSO BI Reporting role is entry level analytics and reporting position within the Marketing Science Operations (MSO) BI team. This role is ideal for candidates who are interested in building strong foundations in business intelligence, reporting, and data quality.

The team will provide structured learning and on‑the‑job exposure. What matters most is curiosity, critical thinking, and a strong learning mindset.

LIVE | WHAT YOU WILL DO

As an MSO BI Reporting Associate, you will work under the guidance of Senior Associates and Managers to support BI reporting, dashboarding, and data quality operations.

You will:


Support development and maintenance of standard BI dashboards and reports.
Perform data validation, reconciliation, and QA checks to ensure accuracy and consistency.
Assist in data extraction, transformation, and preparation for reporting use cases.
Monitor data refreshes, pipeline health, and reporting completeness.
Help document metric definitions, data sources, and reporting logic.
Support regular weekly and monthly reporting cycles.
Identify data issues, anomalies, or gaps and escalate appropriately; Closely collaborate with Data Engineering stakeholders.


Basic Qualifications


Bachelor's (3 to 6 Yrs) or master's (2 to 4 Yrs) degree required in any discipline with strong record of academic success in quantitative and analytic coursework such as operations research, applied mathematics, management science, data science, statistics, econometrics, or engineering.
Experience in Tableau, Power BI, or similar visualization tools; Familiarity with dashboards, KPIs, or recurring reports
Effective oral and written communication skills.
Comfort working with structured data, tables, and reports.


Preferred Qualifications


Basic experience with Excel, SQL, or any BI/reporting tool
Interest in data governance, reporting standards, or analytics operations.
Experience supporting analytics in pharma, healthcare, or regulated environments.
Effective communication skills with the ability to collaborate closely with Senior Associates to deliver high‑quality analytical outputs.
Exposure to DevSecOps practice
Exposure to Incident Management

title: Associate Data Scientist (Commercial)
description:
Associate Data Scientist (L3) – Commercial (Gen Med, AIN)

HOW MIGHT YOU DEFY IMAGINATION?

If you feel like you’re part of something bigger, it’s because you are. At Amgen, our shared mission—to serve patients—drives all that we do. We are global collaborators who achieve together—researching, manufacturing, and delivering ever-better products that reach millions of patients worldwide. It’s time for a career you can be proud of.

Role Overview

The Associate Data Scientist is an entry-level role supporting data science initiatives across Amgen’s General Medicine (Gen Med) portfolio. This role will work closely with Data Science Leads and senior team members to contribute to analytics, modeling, and insight generation, while building strong foundations in applied data science.

Live | What you will do


Support analytics and data science projects across commercial use cases in the Gen Med portfolio
Assist in data preparation, exploratory analysis, and feature engineering to enable modeling efforts
Contribute to development and testing of predictive models and analytical frameworks under guidance of senior data scientists
Perform metric tracking, performance analysis, and validation checks with high attention to detail
Support creation of insight summaries, visualizations, and presentations for business stakeholders
Maintain and update code, dashboards, and analytical assets in Python/SQL environments
Follow best practices for documentation, QA, and reproducibility
Collaborate with team members to meet project timelines and communicate progress effectively
Engage in continuous learning across predictive modeling, experimentation, and commercial analytics methods


Thrive | What you can expect


Structured learning and hands-on experience in commercial data science applications
Exposure to end-to-end analytics delivery across brands and use cases
Mentorship from experienced data scientists and opportunities to grow into independent contributors
A collaborative environment focused on learning, impact, and development


Basic Qualifications


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Engineering, or a related quantitative field
1–3 years of experience in analytics, data science, or related roles
Working knowledge of Python and SQL
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
Attention to detail and ability to deliver high-quality, accurate work


Preferred Qualifications


Exposure to predictive modeling, experimentation, or statistical analysis
Familiarity with commercial analytics or marketing performance measurement
Interest in healthcare, pharma, or life sciences analytics
Experience with tools such as Jupyter notebooks, Git, or data visualization platforms
Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams and senior stakeholders
Strong communication skills and willingness to learn in a fast-paced environment

title: Process Associate- Fresher
description:
Job Title: Process Associate (Fresher)

Location: Hyderabad, Telangana

Company: Harmony United Medsolutions Pvt. Ltd.

About Us

Harmony United Medsolutions Pvt. Ltd. (HUMS) is a dynamic and innovative company dedicated to transforming the healthcare industry. We take pride in being a reliable Healthcare Management partner. With several years of experience, we have perfected our end-to-end services in medical billing, A.R. management, and other essential healthcare operations.

We provide services to Harmony United Psychiatric Care, a US-based Healthcare Company. Our commitment to excellence, innovation, and continuous growth drives our success. As we expand, we are seeking enthusiastic and responsible freshers to join our team as Process Associates.

Position Overview

The Process Associate (Fresher) will support employee attendance tracking, workplace monitoring, and operational documentation. This is an entry-level role suitable for candidates who are willing to learn, follow company policies, and work closely with HR, IT, and Security teams.

Key Responsibilities

Attendance Monitoring


Assist in tracking daily employee attendance
Support verification of punctuality, absences, and late arrivals
Help generate daily, weekly, and monthly attendance reports
Share reports with HR and management teams


CCTV & Surveillance Support


Assist in monitoring CCTV systems to ensure workplace safety
Report suspicious activities or issues to the concerned team
Maintain basic logs of surveillance recordings


Login/Logout Monitoring


Support tracking of employee login and logout times
Maintain shift-wise working hour records
Identify basic discrepancies and report them to HR


Alarm & Access Control Support


Assist in maintaining alarm system codes and access permissions
Update access lists under supervision
Follow security procedures as per company policy


Operational Support


Assist HR and Operations teams with attendance-related tasks
Maintain records related to attendance and employee logs
Support onboarding activities such as system access setup
Assist during audits when required


Reporting & Documentation


Maintain organized records of attendance and login/logout data
Prepare reports under supervision
Provide required documents during internal reviews


Support & Coordination


Coordinate with HR, IT, and Security teams as needed
Provide support to employees regarding attendance queries
Follow instructions from supervisors and management


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in any discipline (Mandatory)
Freshers are welcome to apply
Basic computer knowledge (MS Excel, MS Word)
Good communication skills
Strong attention to detail
Willingness to learn and work in night shifts
High level of integrity and confidentiality


Diversity, Equality, and Inclusion

Diversity, equality, and inclusion are fundamental to our success at HUMS. We actively promote diversity across all aspects of our organization and foster an inclusive culture where everyone has equal opportunities to grow and succeed.

Work Schedule & Benefits


Competitive salary (including EPF and ESIC)
Health insurance
Four-day work week (Monday – Thursday)
Opportunities for career growth and professional development
Food and cab-drop facilities available
Night Shift: 4:45 PM to 3:45 AM
Work Mode: Work From Office only


Application Process

Please submit your resume detailing your academic background and interest in this role.

For any queries, please contact:

📧 recruitment@hupcfl.com

📞 7780502143

Note: Candidates are requested to contact between 4:45 PM to 3:45 AM IST, Monday through Thursday.
